findings:
Today's report (4/26) drives a heavy cluster of high-relevance validations across the past-week prediction surface. **Eight predictions hit Relevance 5**: indirect-prompt-injection-as-top-CVE-category (4/19) is triple-confirmed by SGLang CVE-2026-5760 + Pillar Antigravity + Google IPI 32% YoY; the local-first-cloud-overflow inversion (4/20) is now codified explicitly in today's own Future #3 as a "consumer-GPU coding agent ≥30% by Q3 2026" thesis; the GGUF-supply-chain prediction (4/21) lands decisively with PoC + CERT/CC for SGLang's chat_template SSTI; the hyperscaler-frontier-lab 5-pole regime (4/21) becomes Big-3-confirmed with the Google × Anthropic $40B / 5GW deal completing the trio; the proprietary-vs-open-weight split (4/21) sharpens via GPT-5.5 API +2x vs DeepSeek V4-Pro at ~7x cheaper landing the same window; the agent-registry / OAuth-trust / Agent-Control-Plane cluster (4/19, 4/22, 4/23) all converge on Okta's Showcase-2026 blueprint with Agent Gateway as central MCP control plane; the 27B-dense + 1M-context standard (4/23) is decisively delivered by Qwen3.6-27B beating a 397B-A17B MoE on SWE-bench Verified at consumer-GPU price points; the cloud-capacity-capture three-cloud-oligopoly thesis (4/25) is the keystone validation of the day.

**Four predictions hit Relevance 4**: secret-leakage-through-CI-and-agents (4/20) extends via Pillar's third agentic-IDE prompt-injection RCE; Physical-AI-as-RaaS (4/22) sustains through Tesla / BMW / Hannover-Messe; 1M-context-default-open-weight (4/24) rides DeepSeek V4 + Qwen3.6-27B; physical-AI-8-hour-production (4/24) and IROS / GTC league-table thesis (4/25) both carry forward momentum; MCP-protocol-attack-surface (4/25) lands both threat (SGLang / Antigravity) and defense (Okta Agent Gateway / TrueFoundry) at the same time.

**Five predictions sit at Relevance 1-2**: 1-bit native training (4/19), Headless Everything (4/20), and Training-vs-inference SKU split (4/22) see no direct progress today (1-bit positioning carries only via the Local LLM Hardware Guide; Bedrock AgentCore's OS-level direction is opposite to headless; no MAIA / MTIA dual-track announcements). Third-party-vendor-boundary / URL-naming-entropy (4/23) and the SaaS-displacement-valuation reset (4/24) both register weak signals: Antigravity is internal-Google not third-party-vendor, and today's market action is hardware-rally driven (NVIDIA $5T retake / AMD +13.9% / Intel +24%) rather than the predicted Workday / Atlassian / Box / Smartsheet 15%+ chained falls.

The dominant structural picture: the same week consolidates **(a)** the three-cloud / three-frontier-lab capital-and-compute coupling, **(b)** the AI-infrastructure CVE class as a real attack-surface category (SGLang second weaponized primitive after LMDeploy), and **(c)** the consumer-GPU coding-agent baseline (Qwen3.6-27B + Ollama MLX + DeepSeek V4-Pro pricing) — these three threads, predicted at different points across 4/19-4/25, all snap into place on 4/26.
relation_to_my_preds:
Two of three personal predictions get strong corroboration today.

**Malicious local LLMs functioning like malware → strong corroboration.** The SGLang CVE-2026-5760 case is exactly the failure mode you described: a model file (GGUF) carries executable code (Jinja2 SSTI in `tokenizer.chat_template`) that fires inside the inference server, and the public PoC at `github.com/Stuub/SGLang-0.5.9-RCE` plus CERT/CC VU#915947 makes it weaponized rather than theoretical. CERT/CC reports no upstream patch was obtained during coordination, which strengthens the "fundamentals must be zero-trust" half of your prediction — defense via behavior monitoring would not have caught the SSTI render. The Okta blueprint at Showcase 2026 explicitly applies zero-trust / multi-layer authentication for data access to agents (Agent Gateway as central MCP control plane, credential vaulting + auto-rotation, runtime enforcement via universal logout), and TrueFoundry's "MCP Security: Guide to Zero Trust for Agentic AI" formalizes this — the "intentionally design system paths AI alone cannot access" architecture is becoming a real product category. On the harness-engineering side, Anthropic's Apr 23 postmortem on Claude Code (March 4 reasoning cut + Apr 16 verbosity-prompt issue) and the Apr 24-25 v2.1.118-120 24-hour regression cluster suggest behavior-spec-as-defense fragility — even legitimate harness changes silently degrade quality, consistent with your "monitor and manage will attract only short-term attention" framing.

**Cloud-API-for-advanced + local-LLM-for-routine division → direct codified confirmation.** Today's Future prediction #3 explicitly says "the consumer-GPU coding agent displaces the cloud-only coding API for ≥30% of dev workloads by Q3 2026" with the 3-corner squeeze of Qwen3.6-27B + Ollama MLX + DeepSeek V4-Pro 75%-off API as the driver. The pricing-structure-shift half of your prediction also lands: GPT-5.5 API at $5 input / $30 output is a 2x increase vs GPT-5.4 (with Codex cutting output tokens ~40% to "offset the per-token raise via per-task cost") — the SaaS-pricing-structure change you called out as a leading signal, appearing the same week DeepSeek V4-Pro shipped at ~7x cheaper than Claude Opus 4.7. Tesla's Q1 2026 capex revision above $25B (cited in the carry-forward stock section) is a parallel data point on the AI compute cost curve.

**RL/LLM-based forecasting performance for real-world phenomena → no direct hit today.** Today's news has no items on RL-for-forecasting research, finance/weather/policy LLM forecasters, or methodology benchmarks for LLM-driven prediction systems. The closest adjacent is the Anthropic engineering postmortem (which is a quality-regression analysis, not a forecasting-improvement piece). Carry-only.
